channel.title: Factorio Science Packs — Complete Recipes & Ratios Guide on Factorio Guide
channel.description: Recent content in Factorio Science Packs — Complete Recipes & Ratios Guide on Factorio Guide

item.title: Chemical Science Pack — Blue Science Guide
item.description:
Blue science is the first real complexity spike in Factorio. It's not just about adding a new ingredient — it introduces fluids, cracking, and multi-step production chains. I've seen countless factory tours stall right here because the oil setup wasn't thought through.

TL;DR: Blue science needs oil processing for plastic and sulfur. Build 2 refineries, 1 sulfur plant, 2 plastic plants, and 5 science assemblers. The bottleneck is almost always petroleum throughput.

Recipe and Requirements

Each chemical science pack needs three components:

item.title: Red Science Pack — Automation Science Guide
item.description:
Recipe

1xIron gear
+
1xIron plate
→
1xAutomation science

Assembler Math

Production Goal Assemblers Needed Notes
1 SP (science pack) per second 1 assembler Slow, hand-fed
5 SPM (sustainable) 5 assemblers Needs iron plate belt
10 SPM 10 assemblers + 1 gear assembler Typical early game

Tip: Red science is cheap. Don't overthink it. 10 assemblers producing red science is more than enough for the entire early game research queue.

How to Automate

  1. Belt iron plates into the assembler
  2. Belt iron gears into the assembler (or hand-craft a few stacks)
  3. Use an inserter to pull completed science packs into a chest or directly into lab input
Common mistake: New players often forget that labs need both science packs AND power. Check that your lab has an inserter feeding it!

Research Priority After Red Science

  1. Steel processing — unlocks steel plates
  2. Engine — unlocks engine units (needed for blue science)
  3. Automation 2 — unlocks electric mining drill
  4. Logistics — unlocks green science (electronic circuit + iron gear)

Next: Green Science Guide — the second tier.
